title: Mtambuwa Primary School in orientation session on the BeFit program
author: Mussa Kabichi
body: The In a move aimed at strengthening community ownership of digital learning, Village Development Committee (VDC) members at Mtambuwa Primary School in Boma Zone, Mangochi District, alongside the Primary Education Advisor (PEA), the head teacher, and school coordinators, participated in an orientation session on the BeFit program, where they were introduced to the initiative, interacted with its learning materials, and experienced a live session to better understand its delivery in schools. Facilitators highlighted that the engagement was critical in ensuring the sustainability of the program, particularly as Edukans, the Implementation Support Partner (ISP), prepares to phase out its direct involvement. VDC members were therefore encouraged to take ownership of the initiative and ensure its continuity within the community. To sustain the program, the committee was advised to incorporate BeFit activities into their local development plans and explore various resource mobilisation strategies, including engaging well-wishers for support. During the discussions, the committee acknowledged a number of challenges affecting the implementation of BeFit sessions, notably the lack of headsets, which has led to noisy and less effective learning environments. In response, the VDC chairperson said the committee will work with the community to address the challenge by encouraging the purchase of headsets for learners, thereby improving the quality of sessions. Building Education Foundation Through Innovation and Technology (BEFIT) Program, a Government of Malawi-led program, is dedicated to strengthening foundational literacy and numeracy for learners in Standards 1 to 4 across public primary schools nationwide. By utilizing specialized applications on Android tablets, the program delivers personalized, self-paced learning experiences that cater to each learner’s unique needs and pace of progress

title: St. Augustine 1 Primary School enhancing digital learning
author: Mussa Kabichi
body: Community leaders at St. Augustine 1 Primary School in St. Augustine Zone, Mangochi District, has taken a step towards enhancing digital learning after participating in an orientation session on the BeFit program held on Wednesday.  The session brought together Village Development Committee (VDC) members, the Primary Education Advisor (PEA), the head teacher, and school coordinators, who were introduced to the initiative and given an opportunity to explore its learning materials, including a demonstration of how lessons are delivered in classrooms. Officials facilitating the training stressed the importance of local involvement in sustaining the program, especially as Edukans, the Implementation Support Partner (ISP), is gradually withdrawing from direct support. The meeting, therefore, focused on empowering the community to take a leading role in ensuring the program continues effectively. Participants were further encouraged to integrate BeFit activities into their development plans and identify practical ways of mobilising resources, such as seeking support from partners and well-wishers, to keep the initiative running. Building Education Foundation Through Innovation and Technology (BEFIT) Program, a Government of Malawi-led program, is dedicated to strengthening foundational literacy and numeracy for learners in Standards 1 to 4 across public primary schools nationwide. By utilizing specialized applications on Android tablets, the program delivers personalized, self-paced learning experiences that cater to each learner’s unique needs and pace of progress.
